intro_script: Welcome back! It's July 13th, 2026. We’ve got twenty-seven AI updates today, covering everything from new rules for fertility donors to big tech rethinking their Instagram tools, plus major hardware wins.
outro_script: And that’s your AI digest for July 13th, 2026. Remember to check those ethical guidelines! Thanks for tuning in; keep learning, and have a wonderfully intelligent day ahead.

items.title: Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group
items.source: The Download from MIT Technology Review
items.summary: A European fertility organization advocates for implementing international limits on the number of children a single sperm donor can contribute to. This concern stems from instances where individuals discover having many half-siblings due to anonymous donations. The issue highlights the impact of donor conception on individuals' sense of self and family structure. Researchers are also developing "world models" to help artificial intelligence understand physical reality, representing a new AI frontier.
items.audio_script: From The Download from MIT Technology Review, "Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group." This report details calls by a European organization for establishing international boundaries regarding the volume of children one sperm donor can contribute to. Concerns are drawn from situations where multiple individuals discover extensive unrelated half-sibling relationships originating from anonymous donations, pointing to impacts on individual identity and established family structures. Concurrently, research efforts are advancing "world models" designed to help artificial intelligence comprehend physical reality, marking a progression in AI development. This push toward embodied understanding suggests a significant shift in how sophisticated AI systems will model real-world interactions.

items.title: Nokia Jumps After Defense AI and Optical Networking Orders
items.summary: Nokia shares surged nearly 10% after announcing a defense AI partnership with NestAI for NATO operational needs. The collaboration offers integrated capabilities like AI-enabled command and control on 5G networks. Additionally, Nokia secured approximately €1 billion in optical network orders related to AI and cloud infrastructure. These developments signal that the company's focus is shifting from traditional telecom hardware to AI infrastructure plays. Investors reacted positively, leading analysts to reassess Nokia as an optical networking peer rather than just a legacy vendor.
items.audio_script: From aibusinessweekly.net, "Nokia Jumps After Defense AI and Optical Networking Orders," detailing a significant stock surge following announcements regarding defense artificial intelligence partnerships and substantial optical network contract wins. The company announced a new collaboration with NestAI to provide integrated capabilities, including AI-enabled command and control, specifically designed for NATO operational requirements utilizing 5G networks. Furthermore, Nokia reported securing approximately one billion euros in orders pertaining to optical networking solutions essential for advancing both AI processing and cloud infrastructure buildouts. These developments indicate a pronounced strategic pivot for the corporation, moving emphasis from conventional telecommunications hardware toward critical artificial intelligence infrastructure segments, prompting analysts to reclassify its market standing.

items.title: Meta Pulls Instagram AI Tool Over User Consent Backlash
items.summary: Meta removed its Muse Image AI feature from Instagram after facing significant backlash over its default settings. The tool allowed users to generate AI photos using prompts referencing public accounts without notifying the account owners or obtaining their explicit consent. Criticism focused specifically on this lack of consent architecture, as individuals could have their photos used without warning. In response to feedback and industry critique, Meta stated the feature was withdrawn because it "missed the mark." The reversal highlights heightened user skepticism regarding AI's use with personal likenesses.
items.audio_script: From aibusinessweekly.net, "Meta Pulls Instagram AI Tool Over User Consent Backlash." The report details Meta's decision to remove the Muse Image AI feature from Instagram following substantial negative reaction concerning its default operational settings. This tool enabled users to generate artificial images via prompts that referenced public accounts without notifying the account owners or obtaining explicit permission first. Criticism centered precisely on this absence of a verifiable consent mechanism, allowing personal photos to be utilized without prior warning to the subject. In direct response to feedback and industry scrutiny, Meta issued a statement confirming the feature's withdrawal because it had "missed the mark." This reversal underscores growing user apprehension regarding artificial intelligence applications utilizing individual likenesses within social media platforms.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Theft IP
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI alleging trade secret theft. The company claims OpenAI misappropriated its intellectual property to develop its own consumer hardware. These accusations follow a high-profile partnership established in 2024 when ChatGPT was integrated into the iPhone's operating system. Apple specifically alleges that former employee Tang Tan directed interviews to extract confidential information from current employees. Furthermore, Apple alleged coaching for departing staff to evade security and cited theft of an Apple laptop by former employee Chang Liu.
items.audio_script: From cnbc.com, "Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Theft IP." Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI concerning allegations of trade secret misappropriation related to intellectual property use for consumer hardware development. The complaint follows the integration of ChatGPT into the iPhone’s operating system during a major partnership established in 2024. Specifically, Apple alleges that former employee Tang Tan orchestrated interviews intended to extract confidential data from current personnel. Additionally, the lawsuit details allegations of coaching departing staff on how to bypass security protocols and cites the theft of an Apple laptop by another former employee named Chang Liu. The filing introduces new legal scrutiny regarding the transfer of proprietary knowledge between major technology firms developing advanced AI capabilities.

items.title: Donor-Conceived People Find Hundreds Of Unknown Siblings
items.summary: A man conceived using anonymous donor sperm in the Netherlands discovered his unknown sibling count due to destroyed records from 2004. Other donor-conceived individuals report finding dozens or even hundreds of siblings through genetic testing and registries. This unexpected discovery prompts calls for international limitations on how many children a single donor can contribute. The ease of tracing genetics, despite bans on anonymity in some regions, has revealed vast unknown familial connections. These revelations have raised concerns regarding the physical risks to potential offspring from highly prolific donors.
items.audio_script: From technologyreview.com, "Donor-Conceived People Find Hundreds Of Unknown Siblings." The story reports that individuals conceived using anonymous donor sperm are discovering extensive unknown familial connections through genetic testing and existing registries. One man in the Netherlands found multiple unrecorded siblings because records from 2004 were destroyed; other individuals have reported finding dozens or even hundreds of relatives through these advanced tracing methods, prompting international discussions regarding donation limits. These revelations necessitate heightened attention on potential physical risks associated with highly prolific donors across various jurisdictions.

items.title: Waze rolls out new customization features and more Gemini updates
items.summary: Incorporates new customization options along with Gemini updates. Features enhancements for users navigating on motorcycles specifically through a new "Waze motorcycle mode." These updates aim to improve the overall user experience across various platforms. The roll-out suggests an increased focus on tailored navigation tools. Overall, Waze is expanding its functionality with both personalization and AI integration.
items.audio_script: From Google / Google AI Keyword Blog, "Waze rolls out new customization features and more Gemini updates." This report details recent enhancements to the Waze navigation application, focusing on user personalization and integration with advanced artificial intelligence capabilities. The update introduces new avenues for tailoring the user experience across various platforms, most notably through a dedicated "Waze motorcycle mode" designed specifically to enhance route guidance for two-wheeled vehicles. These additions reflect a systematic effort to deepen personalized navigational tooling within the platform's ecosystem. This ongoing development indicates a trend toward deeper integration of generative AI into daily utility applications across the technology sector.

items.title: Stanford Researchers Introduce TRACE: A Capability-Targeted Agentic Training System That Turns Recurrent Agent Failures Into Synthetic RL Environment
items.summary: Stanford developed TRACE, a system designed to address recurrent failures in agentic LLMs due to missing specific capabilities. TRACE diagnoses these performance gaps by analyzing an agent's own operational trajectories. It then synthesizes one verifiable Reinforcement Learning (RL) environment for each identified capability deficiency. Subsequently, the system trains a specialized LoRA adapter within that synthesized environment. This process significantly improved $\tau^2$-Bench scores by +15.3 points and achieved 73.2% Pass@1 on SWE-bench Verified.
items.audio_script: From MarkTechPost, "Stanford Researchers Introduce TRACE: A Capability-Targeted Agentic Training System That Turns Recurrent Agent Failures Into Synthetic RL Environment." This report details a new methodology that addresses persistent performance gaps in large language model agents by systematically creating targeted training environments. The system, named TRACE, diagnoses capability deficiencies by examining an agent's operational path and subsequently generates verifiable Reinforcement Learning environments corresponding to each identified weakness. Training is then executed using specialized LoRA adapters within these synthetic settings. These advancements resulted in a notable increase of 15.3 points on the $\tau^2$-Bench and achieved a Pass@1 rate of 73.2% on SWE-bench Verified. This development suggests a powerful new paradigm for overcoming inherent limitations in complex AI agent deployment.

items.title: Prime Intellect Releases Verifiers v1: Composable Tasksets, Harnesses, and Runtimes for Agentic RL Training and Evaluations
items.summary: Prime Intellect released verifiers 0.2.0, showcasing a rewritten core in the verifiers.v1 namespace. This new structure modularizes environments into distinct components: a taskset defining the objective (what), a harness detailing execution methods (how), and a runtime specifying the environment location (where). An integrated interception server manages requests while meticulously recording training-ready traces. A key feature is the compatibility allowing any taskset to function under any compatible harness. The release offers full support for prime-rl training from its launch.
items.audio_script: From MarkTechPost, "Prime Intellect Releases Verifiers v1: Composable Tasksets, Harnesses, and Runtimes for Agentic RL Training and Evaluations." The announcement details a significant structural overhaul of Prime Intellect's verifier framework aimed at enhancing agentic reinforcement learning training and evaluation capabilities. This update introduces the verifiers.v1 namespace, which modularizes environments into three distinct components: a taskset defining the objective, a harness specifying execution methods, and a runtime indicating the environment location. Functionality is managed by an integrated interception server that logs training-ready traces, establishing compatibility between any defined taskset and compatible harnesses while ensuring full support for prime-rl training since inception. This structural advancement provides greater flexibility for complex AI simulations across various research applications.

items.title: Meet NeuroVFM: A New Neuroimaging Foundation Model Trained With Vol-JEPA on Uncurated Clinical MRI and CT Volumes
items.summary: NeuroVFM is a generalist neuroimaging foundation model developed by the University of Michigan. It was trained using a volumetric extension, Vol-JEPA, applied to 5.24 million uncurated clinical MRI and CT volumes. The model learns complex brain anatomy and pathology directly from the raw imaging data. A key feature is its ability to learn without requiring specific radiology report labels for training. This represents an advancement in applying foundation models to volumetric medical imaging analysis.
items.audio_script: From MarkTechPost, "Meet NeuroVFM: A New Neuroimaging Foundation Model Trained With Vol-JEPA on Uncurated Clinical MRI and CT Volumes." This report details the introduction of a generalist neuroimaging foundation model that has learned directly from vast amounts of raw clinical scan data. Developed by the University of Michigan, the model utilized a volumetric extension known as Vol-JEPA. Training involved processing five point twenty four million uncurated clinical magnetic resonance imaging and computed tomography volumes. A central aspect of this methodology is its capacity to absorb complex brain anatomical structures and pathological variations without necessitating specific radiology report labels for supervised learning. This represents a measurable step forward in applying comprehensive foundation model techniques to the field of volumetric medical image analysis, suggesting potential shifts in diagnostic support systems.

items.title: Guide to Loop Engineering: How ‘autoresearch’ and ‘Bilevel Autoresearch’ Turn AI Agents Into Autonomous Machine Learning ML Research Loops
items.summary: Loop engineering describes a modern pattern that moves beyond simple manual queries into AI tools. This process utilizes "autoresearch" and "Bilevel Autoresearch" to create autonomous machine learning research loops for AI agents. These concepts are exemplified using Andrej Karpathy’s autoresearch repository. The framework is based on documentation provided by @0xCodila. Such looping mechanisms transform the interaction with AI beyond simple prompt-response cycles.
items.audio_script: From MarkTechPost, "Guide to Loop Engineering: How ‘autoresearch’ and ‘Bilevel Autoresearch’ Turn AI Agents Into Autonomous Machine Learning ML Research Loops." The report details a shift in AI interaction from simple manual querying toward establishing self-directed research processes for artificial intelligence agents. This methodology employs advanced concepts like autoresearch and Bilevel Autoresearch to construct fully autonomous machine learning research cycles, using examples such as Andrej Karpathy's repository framework. These looping mechanisms fundamentally alter the standard prompt-response dialogue model governing AI tool interaction. The implementation signifies a growing capability within the technology sector toward creating self-iterating, knowledge-generating artificial intelligence systems.

items.title: The New York nurses replaced by AI: ‘It should concern every patient who cares about quality of care’
items.summary: A union representing 12 nurses alleges that Montefiore Hospital violated a recently secured contract during layoffs. Marilyn Shuler, a utilization review nurse with 39 years of experience, was among those dismissed on Sunday. These nurses were reportedly replaced by AI-powered software at the facility in the Bronx. NYSNA confirmed the layoffs and indicated significant job displacement due to technological changes. The situation has raised concerns regarding the future quality of patient care.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "The New York nurses replaced by AI: ‘It should concern every patient who cares about quality of care’." This report details allegations that Montefiore Hospital allegedly violated a recently secured contract during layoffs, resulting in the replacement of nursing staff with artificial intelligence software. Twelve unionized nurses filed such an allegation concerning practices at the Bronx facility. Marilyn Shuler, one nurse possessing thirty-nine years of experience, was among those individuals reportedly dismissed on Sunday. New York State Nurses Association confirmed the workforce reductions and highlighted significant job displacement attributable to evolving technology within the medical sector. The situation has subsequently spurred broader discussions regarding projected quality standards in patient care delivery. These documented instances indicate an increasing integration of advanced software solutions into frontline healthcare roles across major metropolitan facilities.

items.title: Is the most popular song played on Australian radio stations the product of generative AI?
items.summary: Josh Fawaz's popular cover of "Like a Prayer" has become a viral sensation on Australian radio charts. Music experts and other musicians are questioning the song's origin. Concerns exist regarding whether generative artificial intelligence was used in its production. The buzz surrounds the possibility that the hit track is AI-generated rather than purely human creation. These questions have sparked discussion about the role of AI in modern music composition.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "Is the most popular song played on Australian radio stations the product of generative AI?". The report details growing speculation surrounding the actual creation methods behind a viral musical success dominating current airplay in Australia. Specifically, the discussion centers on Josh Fawaz’s rendition of "Like a Prayer," which has rapidly climbed the local radio charts, leading music industry experts to examine its potential algorithmic origins. These concerns have ignited wider conversation regarding the increasing influence of artificial intelligence within contemporary musical composition practices. The findings indicate an ongoing necessity for clearer provenance standards in commercially distributed audio content moving forward.

items.title: ‘Navigating the unknown together’: me and my idiot AI boyfriend – podcast
items.summary: An author confronts her initial aversion to artificial intelligence and chatbots while exploring their potential influence. The piece explores themes surrounding AI's place in a "decent society." Through the context of a podcast titled 'Navigating the unknown together', personal skepticism meets technological possibility. This internal conflict suggests a journey from outright repulsion to potential fascination with advanced AI companions, personified as an "idiot AI boyfriend." The writing grapples with the emotional and social implications of increasingly sophisticated artificial intelligence interaction.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "‘Navigating the unknown together’: me and my idiot AI boyfriend – podcast." The piece documents an author's evolving relationship with artificial intelligence chatbots, moving from initial skepticism to a consideration of advanced AI's role in societal structures. The discussion methodically examines the integration of simulated companionship into human life. The author explores her own internal conflict as she engages with sophisticated digital interaction. This personal exploration parallels broader industry conversations regarding emotional reliance on synthetic intelligence. Industry discourse will likely continue to focus on establishing ethical boundaries for increasingly advanced AI companions.

items.title: China’s massive AI rollout - podcast
items.summary: China is rapidly adopting artificial intelligence across numerous sectors, contrasting with Western skepticism. Examples of this widespread adoption include medical avatars and intelligent robots used in factories. The technology extends to daily life through services like food delivery drones operating on the Great Wall of China. Furthermore, the Chinese state has enthusiastically embraced AI for applications such as enhanced surveillance capabilities. These diverse implementations showcase a comprehensive national rollout of artificial intelligence.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "China’s massive AI rollout - podcast." This reporting details China's rapid and widespread integration of artificial intelligence across nearly every major industrial and civilian sector. The nation is showcasing diverse applications ranging from advanced medical avatars to intelligent robotic systems operating within manufacturing facilities. Furthermore, daily routines are being impacted by technology such as food delivery drones utilizing established infrastructure like the Great Wall of China. Beyond commerce, the state has demonstrably adopted AI for enhanced surveillance functions, illustrating a comprehensive national push across multiple domains. These widespread implementations signal an accelerating trend toward deeply integrated governmental and private sector deployment of artificial intelligence technologies.

items.title: Christopher Nolan says people ‘disdain’ AI and the idea it will replace humans is ‘nonsense’
items.summary: Christopher Nolan believes big-budget action films shot on location are resilient even amid artificial intelligence's growth, a technology many people reportedly "disdain." He views anxieties about AI replacing human creative talent as nonsensical. Promoting his upcoming film, an adaptation of *The Odyssey*, Nolan addressed industry concerns surrounding the technology. Separately, he dismissed rightwing criticism directed at Lupita Nyong’o regarding her portrayal of Helen of Troy as irrelevant.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "Christopher Nolan says people ‘disdain’ AI and the idea it will replace humans is ‘nonsense’." Christopher Nolan expressed views on industry anxieties surrounding artificial intelligence's integration into filmmaking. During a promotional address for his forthcoming film adaptation of *The Odyssey*, Nolan stated that large-scale action cinema filmed on location retains its inherent value, while dismissing concerns over AI replacing human creative capacity as unfounded speculation. Furthermore, the discussion included Nolan addressing external criticism directed toward Lupita Nyong’o's performance as Helen of Troy, which he deemed irrelevant to the film's scope. These comments suggest a continuation of industry dialogue emphasizing human artistic control amidst rapid technological advancement in media production processes.

items.title: ‘These are some of the most complex structures ever created’: how tech reporting moved into the physical world
items.summary: Tech reporting is increasingly moving from digital coverage to investigating the physical infrastructure supporting the AI revolution. Journalists now engage in extensive "shoe-leather reporting" concerning these large-scale facilities. A recent Guardian investigation revealed that an £8.2bn AI complex in rural Scotland misrepresented its plans for entirely renewable energy sourcing. Coverage highlights that building datacentres involves significant, tangible physical constraints and realities. These concrete elements are crucial factors determining the success or failure of the AI boom.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "‘These are some of the most complex structures ever created’: how tech reporting moved into the physical world." The report details a shift in technology journalism towards examining the tangible physical infrastructure underpinning the artificial intelligence revolution. Journalists have increasingly adopted methods described as "shoe-leather reporting," investigating large-scale facilities essential to the AI boom. For instance, an investigation uncovered that an £8.2 billion AI complex situated in rural Scotland provided misleading information regarding its commitment to entirely renewable energy sourcing. The coverage underscores that constructing data centres involves numerous concrete physical constraints, factors critical to determining the viability of this technological expansion. The tangible realities of construction and resource management are proving as crucial as the software advancements themselves for the industry's future trajectory.

items.title: 6 months to live for open models
items.summary: Open-source Artificial Intelligence (AI) faces its most critical evaluation period currently. The ongoing situation serves as a major test regarding the practical viability of openly accessible AI models. These current circumstances directly challenge the sustainability and real-world applicability of open AI frameworks. Success in this period will be crucial for determining the future trajectory of open-source AI development.
items.audio_script: From Interconnects, "6 months to live for open models." The report focuses on the current evaluation period facing open-source Artificial Intelligence, concluding that this situation presents a major test of openly accessible AI model viability. Ongoing efforts are directly challenging the sustainability and real-world applicability inherent in current open AI frameworks. The analysis details how success during this timeframe will be critical for defining the future path of open-source AI development. Industry observers note that these current circumstances mandate rigorous performance testing across varied commercial applications to prove long-term utility outside controlled research environments. This outcome suggests a significant inflection point for decentralized artificial intelligence infrastructure adoption.

items.title: Directly Responsible Individuals (DRI)
items.summary: Directly Responsible Individuals (DRI) is a term originally used at Apple to identify the person ultimately accountable for a project's outcome. The concept has recently been applied to discuss LLM-powered agents within human organizations. The author argues that an AI agent should never be designated as a DRI. This belief stems from the unique human capacity to assume accountability, which machines lack. Supporting this, historical advice suggests computers cannot be held accountable for management decisions.
items.audio_script: From Simon Willison's Weblog, "Directly Responsible Individuals (DRI)" details a concept originally employed at Apple to pinpoint the sole person accountable for a project's final outcome, and suggests AI agents should never receive this designation. The piece reports on the recent application of DRI principles to discussions surrounding large language model-powered agents operating within organizational structures. Specifically, the author contends that accountability remains a uniquely human capability that machines cannot assume, citing historical precedent indicating that technology itself has been deemed incapable of bearing management responsibility for decisions made with its assistance. This suggests an ongoing need for clear human designation of ultimate oversight authority when integrating advanced AI tools into core business processes.

items.title: shot-scraper 1.11
items.summary: Version 1.11 introduces minor improvements, primarily enhancing command option consistency. The server mechanism now waits up to 30 seconds for a target URL connection, replacing the previous one-second delay. A new `--js-file` option allows loading JavaScript from local files, standard input, or GitHub sources, offering an alternative to direct argument passing with `--javascript`. Furthermore, `shot-scraper multi` supports this functionality via a `js_file:` YAML key. Finally, both `shot-scraper javascript` and `shot-scraper html` commands now include a `--timeout` option for standardization.
items.audio_script: From Simon Willison's Weblog, "shot-scraper 1.11." This update delivers several key functional enhancements aimed at improving command consistency and extending source loading capabilities. The server mechanism now increases its waiting period for a target URL connection from one second up to thirty seconds. A notable addition is the introduction of the new --js-file option, which permits JavaScript loading from local files, standard input streams, or GitHub repositories as an alternative method to supplying code via direct arguments with --javascript. Furthermore, shot-scraper multi now incorporates this same functionality through a js_file: YAML key, and both primary commands gain a standardized --timeout option. These improvements refine the tooling's stability and adaptability for automated web content extraction processes moving forward.

items.title: Fable gets another bump
items.summary: Anthropic has extended access to Claude Fable 5 on all paid plans through July 19th and increased the weekly rate limits for Claude Code by 50%. Previously, Anthropic cited compute constraints as the reason for these adjustments. In contrast, OpenAI announced the temporary removal of the five-hour usage limit restriction across its Plus, Business, and Pro plans. OpenAI is also rolling out efficiency improvements to GPT-5.6 Sol, suggesting less usage going forward. An observer noted that OpenAI's recent updates are attracting users due to the uncertainty surrounding Fable access.
items.audio_script: From Simon Willison's Weblog, "Fable gets another bump." The report details recent adjustments in service access and rate limits across major large language model providers. Anthropic has extended access to Claude Fable 5 on all paid plans until July 19th and concurrently raised the weekly usage limits for Claude Code by fifty percent. Previously, Anthropic had attributed these changes to compute constraints. In a separate announcement, OpenAI confirmed the temporary lifting of its five-hour usage restriction across Plus, Business, and Pro tiers while also rolling out efficiency upgrades to GPT-5.6 Sol. Observers suggest that the differing service updates are currently drawing significant user attention within the sector.

items.title: sqlite-utils 4.1.1
items.summary: Version 4.1.1 of sqlite-utils primarily addresses an edge case concerning `ON DELETE` actions within transactions. Specifically, calling `table.transform()` now raises a `TransactionError` if foreign keys with destructive `ON DELETE` actions (like CASCADE) exist while a transaction is active and PRAGMA foreign\_keys is enabled. This prevents unintended data modification caused by dropping old tables during transformation. Additionally, the documentation has been updated to ensure bidirectional cross-referencing between the CLI and Python API sections.
items.audio_script: From Simon Willison's Weblog, "sqlite-utils 4.1.1." This update focuses on improving transaction safety within the library when dealing with foreign key constraints and table transformations. The new version specifically addresses an edge case encountered during operations involving destructive deletion actions within active database transactions. When calling the table transform function, the utility now raises a TransactionError if foreign keys employing such actions, like CASCADE, are present while PRAGMA foreign_keys is enabled, thereby preventing unexpected data loss. Furthermore, the associated documentation has undergone revisions to establish clear, bidirectional cross-references between the command-line interface and the Python API sections for user guidance. These enhancements contribute to a more robust development ecosystem for SQLite manipulation in various applications.

items.title: The Three Dimensions of Custom Agentic Alignment: Purpose, Principles and Practices
items.summary: Proposes a framework to align autonomous agentic AI with overarching enterprise intent. This alignment aims to guarantee consistent behavior across various operational scenarios. The framework structures this alignment around three key dimensions: Purpose, Principles, and Practices. Establishing these three areas helps guide the development and deployment of sophisticated AI agents within organizations. Adopting this multidimensional approach ensures that autonomous agents function consistently according to predefined business goals.
items.audio_script: From Towards Data Science, "The Three Dimensions of Custom Agentic Alignment: Purpose, Principles and Practices." The article introduces a framework designed to guide the alignment of autonomous agentic artificial intelligence with broader enterprise intent. This methodology structures consistent AI behavior across diverse operational scenarios by addressing three distinct yet interconnected areas. Development and deployment of sophisticated AI agents are guided through establishing clear definitions around Purpose, Principles, and Practices within an organization. Adopting this multi-dimensional approach ensures that autonomous agents operate in a manner that remains consistent with pre-established business goals. This framework establishes a necessary standard for governing the advanced integration of independent AI systems into corporate workflows moving forward.

items.title: RAG vs Fine-Tuning Explained: What They Actually Do and When to Use Each
items.summary: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and fine-tuning are distinct techniques for improving large language models, addressing different needs. Fine-tuning modifies the model's underlying weights using domain-specific data to change its style or knowledge base fundamentally. Conversely, RAG enhances responses by retrieving external, up-to-date information before generating an answer, grounding it in provided sources. The decision between them depends on whether the goal is behavioral adaptation (fine-tuning) or access to current/specific facts without retraining (RAG). They are complementary tools rather than competing solutions.
items.audio_script: From Towards Data Science, "RAG vs Fine-Tuning Explained: What They Actually Do and When to Use Each." This report distinguishes between two methods for enhancing large language models, clarifying that they serve different operational needs within AI development. The content details fine-tuning as a process that modifies the core weights of a model using domain-specific data, fundamentally altering its style or knowledge base. In contrast, Retrieval-Augmented Generation, or RAG, improves outputs by sourcing and incorporating external, current information into the answer generation process without requiring retraining of the foundational model. The conclusion indicates that these two techniques are supplementary tools, offering developers distinct pathways for behavioral adaptation versus factual grounding.

items.title: How to Orchestrate 100+ Agents With Claude Code
items.summary: Details instructions for running over one hundred AI agents concurrently using Claude Code. The guide was originally published on the Towards Data Science platform. It addresses the technical methods required for managing such a large-scale, parallel agent system. Readers can learn how to orchestrate this complex setup with specific coding practices.
items.audio_script: From Towards Data Science, "How to Orchestrate 100+ Agents With Claude Code" details a technical methodology for running numerous artificial intelligence agents concurrently. The article outlines the specific coding practices necessary to manage such a large-scale, parallel agent system effectively. The guide focuses on instructing readers through the required technical procedures to successfully execute over one hundred autonomous AI entities simultaneously. This advanced setup demands careful orchestration techniques to maintain system stability across all operational units. Experts note that this work signifies a marked advancement in scalable multi-agent simulation capabilities within the rapidly evolving artificial intelligence sector.

items.title: New method aims to keep kids safe from illegal AI-generated content
items.summary: Developed a new auditing technique to assess generative AI models for dangerous functionalities. This method tests the models' compliance without requiring prompts that illicitly generate prohibited content. Researchers aim for this tool to enhance safety against illegal AI-generated material. The innovation allows developers to proactively identify and mitigate potential risks in AI systems.
items.audio_script: From MIT News - Artificial Intelligence, "New method aims to keep kids safe from illegal AI-generated content." Researchers have developed a novel auditing technique designed to evaluate generative artificial intelligence models specifically for dangerous functionalities without needing to input prompts that could illegally generate prohibited material. The methodology involves testing the systems' compliance through alternative assessment methods. This innovation permits developers to proactively identify and mitigate potential security risks embedded within complex AI architectures before deployment. Consequently, this tool represents a significant step toward enhancing safety protocols across the development landscape of artificial intelligence technology.

items.title: Hello everyone, rate my first own open-source library for Reinforcement Learning -> https://github.com/DenisDrobyshev/reinforce
items.summary: The content features a screenshot of an online forum post. The post title is "Hello everyone, rate my first own open-source library for Reinforcement Learning." items.title: Design Learning Loop
items.summary: Two types of learning loops are presented: human feedback and automated feedback. In a human feedback loop, direct input is provided regarding the quality of retrieval outputs like RAG or SQL. An automated example involves manufacturing alarms considering multiple action pathways. After an action is taken, an agent calculates its efficacy by monitoring metrics such as time to stabilization (TTS). This process allows the agent to create a ranked leaderboard for future recommendations.
items.audio_script: From RL Reddit, "Design Learning Loop," which details two distinct mechanisms for refining artificial intelligence systems through iterative feedback processes. The report distinguishes between human feedback loops and automated feedback mechanisms designed to improve output quality. Human involvement in the loop can provide direct assessment on retrieval outputs, such as those generated by Retrieval Augmented Generation or SQL querying. Conversely, an automated method was illustrated using manufacturing alarms, where an agent measures its effectiveness after executing an action by tracking metrics like time to stabilization. This systematic monitoring enables the agent to generate a ranked leaderboard for future decision recommendations, setting a new standard for continuous system refinement in complex operational environments. These structured feedback frameworks indicate a trend toward more self-correcting and empirically validated AI agents across industrial applications.

items.title: Interval Certifications for Multilayered Perceptrons via Lattice Traversal
items.summary: A theoretical framework is presented connecting adversarial robustness to a lattice traversal problem concerning multilayered perceptrons (MLPs). A sound certification interval $I$ ensures that perturbing an input $\mathbf{x} \in I$ does not change the MLP's output, while a complete certification guarantees prediction change when moving outside $I$. The authors develop lattice traversal operators for a refine & verify iterative scheme, validating both sound maximality and complete minimality. They observe that finding minimum solutions for complete certifications is polynomial in oracle calls, unlike sound certifications which face strong intractability. Furthermore, logarithmic algorithms are provided for optimization problems within symmetric $\ell_\infty$-spheres, validated via the ParallelepipedoNN system.

items.title: CogniConsole: Externalizing Inference-Time Control as a Formal Abstraction for Reliable LLM Interactions
items.summary: CogniConsole proposes that reliable large language model (LLM) interaction depends significantly on explicit inference-time control rather than solely on inherent model capability. This architecture externalizes control into a structured interface blending programmatic coordination with bounded prompt reasoning. By testing the system across $N=489$ probes in an interactive environment, researchers found that increasing structural scaffolding systematically reduced output variance and failure rates regardless of the underlying fixed model. The findings suggest many LLM failures stem from poor control specification, not insufficient ability. Consequently, this work establishes inference-time control as a primary abstraction for designing and evaluating reliable LLM systems.

items.title: GATS: Graph-Augmented Tree Search with Layered World Models for Efficient Agent Planning
items.summary: GATS, a new planning framework, addresses the high computational cost and stochasticity of existing LLM-based agents like LATS and ReAct. It combines systematic UCB1-based tree search with a novel three-layer world model to eliminate runtime LLM calls during inference. This layered model integrates exact symbolic matching (L1), statistical analysis (L2), and LLM prediction for unknown actions (L3). On synthetic tasks, GATS achieved a 100% success rate compared to LATS's 92% and ReAct's 64%. Critically, GATS requires zero LLM calls per task while maintaining superior performance on complex benchmarks.

items.title: Long-Horizon-Terminal-Bench: Testing the Limits of Agents on Long-Horizon Terminal Tasks with Dense Reward-Based Grading
items.summary: Long-Horizon-Terminal-Bench addresses the limitation of current benchmarks by evaluating agents on 46 complex, multi-stage tasks across nine categories, including software engineering and scientific computing. Unlike previous setups that only reward final outcomes, this benchmark decomposes tasks into fine-grained subtasks to provide dense intermediate rewards and partial credit. Tasks require extensive computational time, often spanning minutes to hours, demanding robust long-horizon planning and iterative debugging capabilities. Evaluating 15 frontier models revealed that even the best performers scored relatively low pass rates (e.g., 15.2% at a partial reward threshold of 0.95). The benchmark suggests significant room for improvement in training advanced agents for extended, complex workflows.

items.title: A Formalization of the Mean-Field Derivation of the Vlasov Equation: AI-Assisted Lean Formalization as a Strategy Game
items.summary: A novel approach formalizes deriving the Vlasov equation's well-posedness using an AI-assisted process structured as a "formalization game." In this setup, a mathematician directs an AI assistant to convert LaTeX mathematics into verifiable Lean 4 code. The success criteria include successful compilation, absence of placeholders, and reliance solely on foundational axioms for proof validity. A key outcome was the formalization of the Vlasov equation's existence, uniqueness, stability estimate, and weak solution properties. Furthermore, a significant portion of the resulting optimal-transport machinery proved self-contained and reusable within the broader Mathlib library.

items.title: ARCANA: A Reflective Multi-Agent Program Synthesis Framework for ARC-AGI-2 Reasoning
items.summary: ARCANA is introduced as a collaborative, multi-agent framework designed to solve ARC AGI 2 tasks within strict time and hardware limitations. The system breaks down each task into four iterative steps: perception, hypothesis generation, symbolic execution, and reflective refinement. Specific agents handle object-centric scene graph construction from raw grids, propose diverse DSL programs via a latent policy, and verify candidates using a symbolic executor. A crucial reflective agent generates failure-driven feedback for subsequent attempts. These agents coordinate their actions through a shared differentiable blackboard, guided by a learned meta controller to enhance reasoning efficiency.

items.title: Neuro-Agentic Control: A Deep Learning-based LLM-Powered Agentic AI Framework for Controlling Security Controls
items.summary: Traditional rule-based monitoring fails against modern cyberattacks on operational technology, and LLMs pose safety risks for closed-loop control. A novel neuro-agentic control framework is proposed, merging an LLM planner (like Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite) with a pre-trained Time-Series Foundation Model (TimesFM). This system employs "Counterfactual Physics Injection" to simulate potential interventions in the model's latent space before real-world action, rejecting unsafe proposals. Testing on industrial datasets, the framework outperformed LSTM and TCN baselines by preventing five breaches under stochastic attack scenarios. These results confirm foundation models can act as deterministic safeguards for agentic AI in critical infrastructure.

items.title: L-MAD: A Systematic Evaluation of Multi-Agent Debate Structures in Legal Reasoning
items.summary: Introduces the Legal Multi-Agent Debate (L-MAD) framework to systematically evaluate debate structures for Legal Textual Entailment. L-MAD utilizes distinct expert personas across multiple agents, showing performance improvements up to 8\% over single-agent baselines. Analysis reveals that increasing the agent population reduces inconsistency and enhances accuracy in legal reasoning tasks. Conversely, extending discussion rounds leads to "over-deliberation drift," causing agents to reinforce errors. The findings establish practical boundaries for deploying collaborative multi-agent systems in high-stakes legal settings.

items.title: MedRealMM: A Real-World Multimodal Benchmark for Chinese Online Medical Consultation
items.summary: Introducing MedRealMM, a large-scale benchmark for multimodal online medical consultation derived from de-identified Chinese patient-doctor interactions. It employs a Multimodal Clinical Challenge Point (MCCP) framework to create standardized next-response tasks while maintaining the text-image context of real consultations. Each instance is graded by physicians using rubrics that reward beneficial behaviors and penalize unsafe or contradictory responses. The initial release comprises 5,620 multimodal cases across 64 clinical departments, evaluating various LLMs. Results indicate that image information is crucial for reliable performance, though current frontier models struggle with safety-sensitive error avoidance compared to human physicians.

items.title: KV-PRM: Efficient Process Reward Modeling via KV-Cache Transfer for Multi-Agent Test-Time Scaling
items.summary: Process Reward Models (PRMs) enhance multi-agent systems via test-time scaling but face computational bottlenecks due to expensive re-encoding of long trajectories ($O(L^2)$ cost). KV-PRM addresses this by directly utilizing the existing Key-Value (KV) cache generated during LLM decoding instead of reprocessing text. This approach reduces the scoring complexity from quadratic to linear ($O(L)$), significantly improving efficiency for long contexts. Benchmarking on MATH, GSM8K, and AIME demonstrates that KV-PRM matches or exceeds traditional text-based PRMs across methods like Beam Search and MCTS. Quantitatively, it achieves dramatic reductions, including up to a 5,000x decrease in scoring FLOPs and a 34x reduction in memory footprint.

items.title: A Unified Approach to Interpreting Knowledge Distillation for Large Language Models via Interactions
items.summary: Existing knowledge distillation (KD) methods for Large Language Models (LLMs) lack a clear understanding of their underlying mechanism. This paper proposes analyzing various KD techniques by decomposing LLM output scores into constituent interactions. The core finding is that the common mechanism involves the sparsification of these interactions, where student models zero out certain relationships during inference. Performance variance among KD methods relates to how effectively they handle complex interactions; higher sparsity of complex interactions generally boosts performance. To enforce this, the authors introduce a Complex Interaction Penalty (CIP) loss function, which improves diverse KD methods across various benchmarks.

items.title: iLENS: Interpretable LLM-Guided Mixture-of-Experts for Neuroimaging Survival Analysis
items.summary: Alzheimer's Disease (AD) conversion prediction during prodromal stages requires advanced risk models, but existing survival predictors lack interpretability and natural language reasoning. Introducing iLENS, a framework utilizes an interpretable Large Language Model (LLM) guided Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) approach for AD survival analysis. This method leverages the LLM to synthesize both structured neuroimaging measurements and unstructured clinical information. iLENS guides expert routing while demonstrating competitive predictive performance and robust patient subtyping capability. Crucially, it offers transparent, biologically grounded rationales, improving clinical decision support in survival analysis.

items.title: Signed Symmetric Quantization for Few-Bit Integers
items.summary: Standard symmetric quantizers often suffer quantization errors for positive outliers due to their strictly positive scale assignment when using signed integers. While asymmetric quantization solves this with a zero point, it incurs runtime overhead. Signed symmetric quantization offers a third approach by placing the extra negative representable value on the dominant outlier tail via a lightweight sign selection rule, keeping the zero point at zero. Theoretical analysis proves this signed absmax grid is conditionally bound-optimal for $\ell_2$ error in most LLM weight groups. Empirical tests confirm this method improves perplexity and accuracy compared to standard unsigned quantizers with no extra inference cost.

items.title: Sticky Routing: Training MoE Models for Memory-Efficient Inference
items.summary: StickyMoE introduces a differentiable routing consistency loss designed to improve memory efficiency in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models during inference. This loss penalizes abrupt expert switching between adjacent tokens, encouraging stable expert assignment across semantically related spans. Unlike previous system or post-hoc methods, StickyMoE integrates this constraint directly into the training process without architectural changes. Experiments demonstrated that applying StickyMoE significantly reduces the expert switch rate by up to 60%. This method achieves such gains while maintaining performance comparable to state-of-the-art techniques with minimal perplexity degradation.

items.title: Reward Transport: Property Control in Flow Matching via Noise-Space Alignment
items.summary: Reward Transport reinterprets the coupling in flow matching as an alignment interface by pairing noise vectors with data based on a target molecular property. This method utilizes optimal transport at training time to align a scalar noise-space coordinate with specific molecular rewards. During inference, changing this single coordinate steers the generated distribution without needing external models or gradient calculations. Practically, sweeping this coordinate allows for continuous control over properties like logP and QED across different chemical datasets. The technique demonstrates transferable control, exhibiting opposite structural effects (e.g., growing vs. shrinking molecules) depending on the targeted property.

items.title: Director: Accelerating Distributed MoE Serving via Online Proactive Expert Placement
items.summary: A new distributed MoE serving system named Director improves efficiency by proactively managing expert placement across GPUs. It addresses limitations in current methods by employing an online prediction approach suited for diverse request patterns. Director utilizes a lightweight predictor or quantized replica to forecast incoming request expert activations. An online migration module minimizes downtime by performing necessary changes during computation-heavy phases. Experiments show that using this relaxation-based optimization leads to significant latency reductions of $11\sim55\%$ compared to existing techniques on popular MoE models.

items.title: LieBN: Batch Normalization over Lie Groups
items.summary: Manifold-valued data requires specialized normalization techniques, known as Riemannian normalization, for Deep Neural Networks (DNNs). Existing methods are often limited to specific geometries or fail to normalize sample distributions effectively. This paper introduces LieBN, a framework providing Riemannian Batch Normalization specifically for Lie groups. LieBN utilizes theoretically convenient left- and right-invariant metrics inherent to all Lie groups, offering theoretical control over the Riemannian mean and variance. The proposed method is demonstrated across nine distinct manifold geometries, including novel metrics on the SPD manifold.

items.title: HERO: A Heterogeneity-Aware Benchmark Library for Federated Continual Learning
items.summary: Federated continual learning (FCL) evaluation suffers due to inconsistent variable changes across existing benchmarks. Introducing HERO, a new library, addresses this by separating task split, client data split, and client task sequence into manageable components. HERO offers a core benchmark ($\alpha$ for data skew, $\rho$ for order mismatch) tested on CIFAR-100 and TinyImageNet using multiple metrics. Evaluation confirms that average accuracy masks poor performance among individual clients and that different settings favor distinct learning strategies. The library provides reproducible streams and scripts to enable setting-aware comparison beyond image datasets.

items.title: DaDaDa: A Dataset for Data Pricing in Data Marketplaces
items.summary: Data marketplaces facilitate increasing data transactions, yet accurately pricing unique data products is a major challenge. Traditional economic pricing methods prove inadequate due to issues like near-zero marginal cost or unpredictable revenue streams. To solve this gap, researchers introduce \texttt{DaDaDa}, the first dataset for data product pricing. This dataset contains metadata for 16,147 data products sourced from nine global marketplaces. $\texttt{DaDaDa}$ allows for training robust pricing models and supports other market tasks like classification and retrieval.

items.title: Accelerating GPU Inference of Large Language Models with Moderately Unstructured Sparse Weight Matrices
items.summary: High LLM inference costs necessitate pruning weights into sparse matrices, yet current GPU kernels struggle to outperform dense operations with moderate sparsity levels ($\sim 50\%$). This research introduces an efficient GPU inference method employing a novel three-layer matrix storage format. This format includes Sparse-TC for tensor core acceleration, a Slot-Filling layer for compression, and a Residual Layer for computation integrity. A new SpMM kernel jointly utilizes sparse tensor cores and CUDA cores to create an overlapping execution pipeline. Evaluations demonstrate up to 1.64x speedup over prior state-of-the-art methods like SpInfer on modern GPUs.

items.title: EHR-MPC: Inference-Time Control for Sepsis Treatment with Generative Patient Digital Twins
items.summary: Sepsis management lacks clear optimal treatment protocols, and current reinforcement learning (RL) methods use fixed strategies. EHRMPC addresses this by decoupling patient dynamic learning from treatment optimization using a generative electronic health record (EHR) model as a digital twin. This digital twin predicts patient trajectories under potential interventions. Model predictive control (MPC) then utilizes these predictions to plan optimal treatments during inference time via simulation. Evaluations on an 8-hospital ICU sepsis cohort showed EHR-MPC matched off-policy performance while improving simulated results compared to RL baselines.

items.title: Influence Diagnostics in High-dimensional M-estimation: Precise Asymptotics
items.summary: Leave-one-out influence traditionally measures how removing a single training point affects model accuracy. Understanding this influence is difficult in high dimensions because sample effects become complexly dependent on all other data points. For convex M-estimation under Gaussian design in the high-dimensional limit where $n$ scales with $d$, the distribution of influences converges to a precisely characterized limiting measure. Furthermore, research shows that highly influential samples tend to cluster near the model's decision boundary. This observation provides support for utilizing such patterns within active learning data selection methods.

items.title: Spectrally Deconfounded Gradient Boosting
items.summary: Develop a nonlinear spectral deconfounding framework specifically for gradient boosting methods to address sensitivity to unobserved confounders. The technique replaces standard squared-error loss with a spectral loss, which dampens learning in directions correlated with latent confounder information. Effective deconfounding requires the combined effect of spectral shrinkage and regularization, particularly through early stopping mechanisms. Authors propose an empirical-Bayes procedure for tuning this new spectral loss function. Experimental results demonstrate that spectrally deconfounded boosting substantially improves target function estimation compared to existing nonlinear baselines under hidden confounding.

items.title: Deep Gaussian Processes on Directed Acyclic Graphs
items.summary: Models representing real-world processes often involve function compositions structured as Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs). These models face challenges due to partially observed, noisy, and heterogeneously sampled measurements across the DAG structure. The paper introduces Deep Gaussian Processes over DAGs by placing priors on these underlying functions. Researchers theoretically investigate prior-collapse behavior, determining how graph topology and intermediate observations affect information retention. A structured variational approximation is proposed that maintains graph dependencies and captures collider effects, validated on tasks like protein signaling networks.

items.title: Adaptive Bayes exactly tracks information over intrinsic time
items.summary: Bayesian and multiplicative-weights updates reweight experts or actions based on sequential feedback. These updates obey an exact information-accounting identity for their regret. On any given round, excess loss equals an immediate payment for exposed uncertainty plus a reduction in distance from chosen comparators. Summing these one-step balances reveals two exact adaptive decompositions of cumulative regret. This pathwise accounting framework applies universally across various learning paradigms, including Hedge and online convex optimization.

items.title: A Statistical Test for the Benefits of Personalizing Interventions
items.summary: Addresses the challenge of quantifying the benefit of personalized interventions versus using a single best option across fields like medicine and marketing. Introduces a statistical hypothesis test designed to evaluate if a personalized policy outperforms the best non-personalized approach using historical data. The test maintains strict control over type-I error while achieving asymptotic normality with minimal variance under stated conditions. Demonstrated versatility and superior performance when tested on various datasets, including those from job training and depression treatment. Provides decision-makers in intervention sciences with a simple, powerful measure of personalization's potential added benefit.

items.title: Nonconvex Composite Functional Constraints via First-Order Augmented Lagrangian Methods under Local Regularity
items.summary: Investigates nonasymptotic convergence for primal-dual methods applied to nonconvex optimization problems with convex-composite structures. The method addresses difficulties from constraint violation and unbounded multipliers by restricting the dual variable to an auxiliary compact set. A nonsmooth nonconvex-concave minimax reformulation leads to a finite-time mechanism converting stationarity of the truncated problem into a KKT certificate. Establishing explicit convergence rates, the approach achieves $O(K^{-1/3})$ with dual regularization or a sharper $O(K^{-1/2})$ rate under specific unregularized assumptions. This demonstrates robust analysis for nonconvex functional inequality constraints.

items.title: Stochastic Linear Bandits with Partially Observed Actions
items.summary: Investigates partially observed stochastic linear bandits, where agents only receive random subsets of action coordinates. Since full observability makes sublinear regret impossible, the work focuses on cases with low intrinsic action dimension. A proposed algorithm, TOFU-POV, estimates the latent subspace from masked actions and runs OFUL in these lower dimensions. Theory proves TOFU-POV achieves $\sqrt{T}$ regret scaled by the subspace dimension, not the full ambient dimension. The research also introduces a rank-adaptive method and provides a novel lower bound demonstrating performance guarantees in this partial observation setting.

items.title: Optimal Top-$k$ Identification from Pairwise Comparisons
items.summary: Investigates active learning for identifying the top-$k$ items using noisy pairwise comparisons until a specified error probability $\delta$ is met. The goal is to minimize the expected number of necessary comparisons (sample complexity). While asymptotic optimality exists for some pure exploration problems, an efficient algorithm remains unestablished for top-$k$ identification from these comparisons under latent utility models. Researchers address this by characterizing the lower bound as a saddle-point problem. This structure allows them to develop an online, primal-dual procedure and construct an adaptive algorithm proven to be asymptotically optimal.
